title: platostoma helenae
intro: Platostoma helenae, also known as Helen's platostoma, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the tropical forests of West Africa. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of the plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, headache, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting against bacterial and fungal infections, and exhibiting antioxidant activity, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The leaves and stems of the plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, and can also be used in traditional African folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
